explanations.1: This comment tells Python to ignore type-checking errors in this file, which is useful when the code might not strictly match Python's type system but works fine at runtime.
explanations.54: The code skips data URLs (images encoded as base64 text strings) because they don't need to be downloaded separately—they're already embedded in the HTML.
explanations.60: This requests the image with verify=False, which means it won't check if the SSL certificate is valid; this is a shortcut for development but risky in production.
explanations.62: BytesIO wraps the raw image bytes in a file-like object so that PIL's Image.open() can read it, even though it's coming from the internet instead of disk.
explanations.88: networkidle means the script waits until the page stops making network requests, ensuring all initial content (including lazy-loaded images triggered on page load) is downloaded.
explanations.116: This checks if the HTML tag has loading="lazy" attribute; if it does, the browser will delay loading that image until it's about to appear on screen.
explanations.119: The 'and' operator here requires *both* width and height attributes to exist; if either one is missing, has_dimensions becomes false.
